preset_description: Dark = identity signal. Visual language expresses who the user is. Validator gets authority and weight (heavy dark). Mover gets minimal light with one dark CTA punch. Explorer gets all-neutral — open, spacious, no urgency.

brief.audience: Startups needing an ai-first, intent-aware design system

accent_tokens._note: Brand primary CTA color. Global — not per-preset. Two variants: button on a light/neutral section vs. on a dark section.

beats.name: Orientation
beats.rationale: Establishes context for startup teams exploring an AI-first, intent-aware design system. Sets the scene and signals relevance before any persuasion begins — essential for Explorer archetypes who want to understand what they're looking at before diving deeper.

beats.name: Value
beats.rationale: Explorer archetype guidance requires a Value beat. At the decision funnel stage, visitors already have awareness — they need to see concrete capabilities and outcome promises that differentiate MODE from alternatives they've been evaluating.

beats.name: Credibility
beats.rationale: Explorer archetype guidance requires a Credibility beat. Placing trust signals and social proof after Value reinforces the promises just made, giving the Explorer confidence that MODE delivers on its claims without feeling pressured.

beats.name: Evidence
beats.rationale: Decision funnel stage requires Evidence before Conversion. Metrics, case study data, and proof points give the Explorer the substantive, data-driven validation they need to feel informed and empowered in their decision — not pushed.

beats.name: Decision
beats.rationale: Decision funnel stage requires a Decision beat before Conversion. Pricing and plan comparison framing lets the Explorer self-select the right tier, maintaining their sense of autonomy while providing the clarity needed to commit.

beats.name: Conversion
beats.rationale: Must appear immediately before Recovery. For an Explorer at the decision stage, the CTA should feel like a natural next step rather than a hard close — framed as unlocking access to explore further rather than a high-pressure commitment.

beats.name: Recovery
beats.rationale: Always last. Provides secondary paths (docs, community, demo booking) for Explorers who aren't ready to convert yet, ensuring no visitor hits a dead end and every exit point offers a lower-commitment re-entry into the funnel.

sections.name: Navigation
sections.rationale: Global navigation — wayfinding and persistent CTA for the session.

sections.name: Hero
sections.rationale: Establishes context for startup teams exploring an AI-first, intent-aware design system. Sets the scene and signals relevance before any persuasion begins — essential for Explorer archetypes who want to understand what they're looking at before diving deeper.

sections.name: Features
sections.rationale: Explorer archetype guidance requires a Value beat. At the decision funnel stage, visitors already have awareness — they need to see concrete capabilities and outcome promises that differentiate MODE from alternatives they've been evaluating.

sections.name: Footer
sections.rationale: Page close — brand mark, minimal navigation, and legal.

page.reasoning: Decision stage requires conversion focus. Per notes: 'minimal variant for conversion-focused pages — strip nav_links, keep logo and single CTA only.' The persistent CTA in nav is critical at this funnel stage.

Explorer archetype with decision stage context. Notes state: 'Explorer variant prefers editorial or with-social-proof — discovery tone, breathing room, no pressure toward conversion.' Social proof aligns with organic context and reduces conversion friction for explorers.

slots.headline: Your design system knows how to render. MODE teaches it why.
slots.subhead: MODE adds a semantic intent layer to your component library — resolving behavior, visual emphasis, and copy register to the right experience for every archetype, funnel stage, and context. No manual variants. No QA overhead. No Figma handoff cycles.
slots.trust_signal: One-time purchase. Lifetime access. Runs in your codebase — no vendor dependency.

page.reasoning: Explorer archetype requires discovery-oriented layout. Per notes: 'Explorer variant uses 2-col-heavy with longer copy per feature,' providing breathing room and detailed exploration suitable for intent-aware discovery.

slots.headline: What the intelligence layer actually does
slots.subhead: MODE doesn't add more settings — it adds judgment. Here's what that looks like in practice across the resolution engine, generation pipeline, and token system.

features.title: Semantic resolution engine
features.description: Components resolve behavior across four dimensions simultaneously: archetype, funnel stage, intent state, and context mode. The system determines the right configuration by default — no manual variant branching, no conditional logic written by hand. The intelligence is in the layer, not the component.

features.title: IA planner + multi-variant generation
features.description: Go from a one-line brief to a full page architecture in seconds, powered by Claude Opus. One brief produces Mover, Validator, and Explorer variants in parallel — each correctly weighted for its archetype. What used to be a design sprint is now a starting point.

features.title: Palette map — semantic visual emphasis
features.description: Visual weight accumulates toward conversion automatically. The palette map resolves per component, per funnel stage — so the right elements carry the right visual authority at every step. No manual accent assignment, no token spreadsheets, no designer in the loop for every page.

features.title: Behavioral tokens
features.description: Copy density, evidence requirements, and CTA rules are driven by archetype tokens — not editorial judgment applied case by case. An Explorer gets richer explanations and softer calls to action. A Mover gets directness and urgency. The system knows the difference and applies it consistently.

features.title: Component manifest — 11 components, fully specified
features.description: Every component ships with zone affinity, slot schemas, and usage intent already defined. You know exactly where each component belongs in a page architecture, what content it expects, and why. The manifest is the contract between your design system and the intelligence layer above it.

features.title: Yours to own, extend, and fork
features.description: MODE lives in your codebase, not a vendor dashboard. Built on Radix UI primitives — nothing new to learn. Swap presets, extend the manifest, adapt to your stack. No runtime dependency, no usage-based billing, no lock-in. Running in under an hour, configured once, yours forever.

page.reasoning: Decision stage conversion page requires recovery mechanism. Notes specify: 'with-cta variant recaptures departing users on key conversion pages — use on pricing and signup flows.' Recapturing explorers at footer is critical at decision stage.
